records.ety: [L. epidemus, Gr. <?/, <?/, among the people, epidemic; <?/ in + <?/ people: cf. F. pidmique. Cf. Demagogue.]

defs: Common to, or affecting at the same time, a large number in a community; -- applied to a disease which, spreading widely, attacks many persons at the same time; as, an epidemic disease; an epidemic catarrh, fever, etc. See Endemic.
Spreading widely, or generally prevailing; affecting great numbers, as an epidemic does; as, epidemic rage; an epidemic evil.
quotes: It was the epidemical sin of the nation.
